name: Wheat grass medium (PMC10119117)
description: wheat grass medium — medium reported in PMC10119117, extracted for GrowthDB and mapped to BiGG.

provenance.citation: Medium composition from PMC10119117 (see GrowthDB record).
provenance.notes: Composition mapped to BiGG (mapper + salt dissociation); presence-based bounds; paper_amount retained. Added via GrowthDB curation.
provenance.verification: paper-verified (complete recipe; cited + supplementary sources chased)
provenance.verification_evidence: a concentrated infusion was prepared from wheat grass (GSE Vertrieb GmbH, Germany) at a concentration of 25 g L-1 which was subsequently cleared from larger particles through filtration (0.2 um pore size). 10 mL L-1 of the concentrated infusion were supplemented with a balanced salt solution
provenance.base_medium: Wheat grass medium (Cerophyl-type Paramecium medium) after Krenek, Berendonk & Petzoldt 2011 (Eur J Protistol) and Sonneborn 1970, but with stigmasterol omitted.

sources_chased.what: original wheat grass medium recipe cited as ref 29 (Krenek, Berendonk & Petzoldt 2011, Eur J Protistol 47:124-137, doi:10.1016/j.ejop.2010.12.001)
sources_chased.how: WebFetch doi.org -> Elsevier (linkinghub S0932473910000878) paywalled; WebSearch + furthlab.xyz protocol consulted for context

sources_chased.what: wheat grass infusion preparation cited as ref 30 (Sonneborn 1970, Methods in Paramecium Research, Methods in Cell Biology vol. 4)
sources_chased.how: reference identified in reference list; only supplies the wheat grass infusion step, which is fully re-stated in the target paper main text (25 g/L, 0.2 um filtered, 10 mL/L)

sources_chased.what: supplementary material (MOESM1_ESM.pdf) of the target paper
sources_chased.how: europepmc supplementaryFiles zip -> pdftotext; contains only modeling/results, no medium composition

recuration.notes: The complete recipe used in THIS paper is fully specified in the main text as explicit final concentrations, so nothing had to be recovered from the cited base recipe. The base reference [29] Krenek 2011 is paywalled, but its only additive not already in the main text is stigmasterol, which the authors explicitly did NOT add ('Contrary to the original recipe, no stigmasterol was added') - so stigmasterol is correctly excluded. NOTE ON PROVENANCE: the trace metals in the target's current_components (Co2+, Cu2+, Fe2+, Fe3+, Mn2+, Zn2+) have NO textual support in the defined recipe - the balanced

uncovered.name: Wheat grass infusion
uncovered.note: undefined/complex ingredient; represented elsewhere as an in-silico approximation, no single exchange

oxygen_note: no oxygen requirement specified; O2 available but not asserted
